compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Change #685 pushed (producer signing seam, expand phase): sign_manifest repointed to mint rebar.opcert.v1 Ed25519 op-certs, race-safe key genesis (os.link exclusive-create), read-both dispatch wrapper, degrade path, schema updates. Stacked on #664 (4214). Validated: implementer's 24 AC tests + independent held-out race/verify-never-creates-key oracles (mutation teeth). Awaiting LLM-Review + Verified.

compiled_state.description:
# op-cert: producer signing seam — gates mint Ed25519 op-certs with the environment key (expand phase)

## Why
The gate producers still sign verdicts with the legacy per-clone HMAC secret via the
`signing.sign_manifest` seam. The epic's "Producer-signing & compute-runtime design resolution"
settles fork A — **uniform producer signing**: the producers mint `rebar.opcert.v1` DSSE certs
with the ambient environment's Ed25519 key, so a local run and a trusted-server run produce the
same artifact and there is exactly ONE signature per verdict (no double-signing; the remote
handler never signs separately). This story is the expand phase: write-new (Ed25519 op-certs),
read-both (envelopes + legacy HMAC records).

**Hard dependency (not yet on `main`):** the storage keystone e4df (Gerrit change 660, in review
on the 649←650←660←664 stack) provides `signing.sign_opcert_manifest()` (the envelope-bearing
`SIGNATURE` event) and `opcert.opcert_from_record()`. Neither exists on current `origin/main` —
this story MUST NOT start until that stack lands, and its first AC pins a mechanical import
pre-check so the dependency is enforced, not assumed.

## What
- **The seam is `signing.sign_manifest` itself — a global repoint, not per-site edits.**
  `signing.sign_manifest(ticket_id, manifest, kind=…)` internally mints a `rebar.opcert.v1`
  DSSE op-cert (via the e4df `sign_opcert_manifest` machinery), deriving bound values when the
  caller does not supply them: material fingerprint from the canonical manifest (the kind's
  existing material where present), bound commit from the manifest's `verified-at-sha` line,
  else current `HEAD`. Every existing caller inherits Ed25519; the full blast radius is
  enumerated and each site gets a test:
  - `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py:333` (`sign_plan_review` — the plan-review producer);
  - `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py:538` (`refresh_attestation` — the drift-refresh
    producer; MUST re-sign as an op-cert so a refresh never downgrades a certified op-cert back
    to an HMAC record in the last-writer-wins attestations map);
  - `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/resign.py:126` (`resign_plan_review`);
  - `src/rebar/signing.py:637` (the `rebar sign` CLI);
  - `src/rebar/_lib_writes.py:638` → `src/rebar/_mcp_writes.py:245` (the MCP `sign_manifest`
    tool).
  After this story no caller HMAC-signs these kinds; the contract story (HMAC removal) then
  deletes the dead HMAC branch.
- **Environment key genesis** (`src/rebar/signing.py`): on first signing use, auto-generate a
  **passphrase-free Ed25519 keypair** at `<tracker>/.opcert-key` / `.opcert-key.pub`
  (`ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -N ""`, key file 0600, git-ignored) — mirroring the HMAC
  `.signing-key` genesis it replaces. Genesis is atomic + race-safe with `ssh-keygen`'s two-file
  reality (`ssh-keygen -f <path>` always writes `<path>` AND `<path>.pub`; it cannot write to
  an fd): generate into a private `mkdtemp` 0700 staging dir (`ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -N "" -f
  <staging>/key`), then `os.link` the PRIVATE key to `<tracker>/.opcert-key` as the SINGLE
  exclusive-create commit point (`os.link` fails with `EEXIST` if a concurrent first-signer
  won; never `os.replace`, which is not exclusive): the loser ADOPTS the winner's key by
  re-reading the existing file. `.opcert-key.pub` is DERIVATIVE, never a commit point — written
  from the committed private key (`ssh-keygen -y`) and re-derivable any time it is missing.
  The staging dir is removed in a `finally`. DSSE principal/keyid defaults to the
  environment's existing `env_id`; `REBAR_OPCERT_ENV_ID` overrides (deployment config).
  Verify-side key resolution never creates a key.
- **Key-loss pre-mortem:** a deleted/unreadable key file is regenerated on the next sign (a
  fresh keypair under the same `env_id`); certs signed by the lost key then read as
  not-certified on verify and are re-earned on the next gate run — the same lifecycle as any
  stale attestation, no operator ceremony. Only when regeneration itself is impossible
  (`ssh-keygen` unavailable, tracker directory unwritable) does signing take the degrade path
  below. (Pinned trusted-environment keys are different:
  rotation there follows the epic's era protocol in `.rebar/trusted_environments.yaml`.)
- **Close-gate producer**: the completion-verifier sign call in
  `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py` rides the same repointed seam; the never-sign
  structural guards (non-PASS, degraded coverage), the material-drift recheck, and the
  `certifiable: false` / `source=="local"` withhold rules are all preserved unchanged.
- **Read path (expand) — the dispatch rule is explicit.** `signing.verify_record` (the single
  verify chokepoint `verify_signature` and `compute_validity` sit on) dispatches on the RECORD
  SHAPE. Mechanically, the dispatch lives in a shape-aware wrapper (e.g.
  `verify_attestation_record(record, ticket_id, *, repo_root)`), because the existing
  `verify_record(record, ticket_id, key)` takes the raw HMAC secret and cannot carry an op-cert
  trust root: legacy `signature`-bearing records route to the UNCHANGED
  `verify_record` + HMAC secret; `envelope`-bearing records route to
  `attest.registry.verify(OPCERT_KIND, envelope, trust_root)` — matching `registry.verify(kind,
  envelope, trust_root)` — under the substrate's OWN kind (the `Policy(SshsigScheme,
  namespace=OPCERT_NAMESPACE)` entry `opcert.register_opcert_policy()` registers;
  `OPCERT_KIND = OPCERT_NAMESPACE = "rebar.opcert.v1"`, the constants in `attest/opcert.py`,
  giving domain separation from `rebar.authorship.v1`), with `trust_root` built from the
  environment's own public key via `authorship.allowed_signers_from_keys([own_pub],
  principal)`. EVERY `verify_record` caller moves to the wrapper — the complete inventory (grep-verified):
  `signing.verify_signature` (signing.py:675), `signing.verify_attestations` (signing.py:692 —
  the all-kinds iterator), and `_engine_support/validate.py:117` (`signature_findings`). The
  wrapper's translation rule for envelope records is a LITERAL table (downstream readers see
  the existing contract unchanged; `key_id` carries the record's `principal`, which keeps
  `drift_refresh_candidate`'s provenance read — `llm/plan_review/attest.py:529`,
  `result.get("key_id")` — working as-is):
  - `record.principal != own env_id` → the WRAPPER returns `verdict: "foreign_key"`,
    `certified: false` WITHOUT invoking the scheme (there is no key to verify with — exactly
    today's HMAC `foreign_key` semantics; `SshsigScheme` itself never emits `foreign_key`);
  - `record.principal == own env_id` → `registry.verify(OPCERT_KIND, envelope, root)` and the
    `Verdict` maps `verified → certified`, `verdict → verdict` (pass-through), `reason →
    reason`. The strings this path can emit, verbatim from the code: `"certified"`,
    `"mismatch"`, `"invalid"` (malformed principal/namespace), `"unavailable"` (ssh-keygen
    missing — `sshsig.py:110`), plus the registry's defensive `"unknown_kind"` /
    `"unknown_scheme"`. (`"key_not_valid_at_era"` is emitted only by the merge-gate era path,
    `opcert.verify_opcert`, and is already in the schema enum.) The attestation kind's
  legacy POLICY pins are NOT touched: `POLICY["plan-review"]` / `POLICY["completion-verifier"]`
  remain HMAC-pinned through the expand phase and are removed by the contract story (8f1d). The
  envelope is verified against the environment's own public key
  (`.opcert-key.pub`) for same-environment verification — a cert whose `principal` is another
  environment is classified `foreign_key` BY THE WRAPPER per the translation table below (the
  scheme is never invoked for it); a record carrying
  the legacy `signature` (HMAC hex) field routes to the unchanged HMAC path (removed later by
  the contract story). The event/record plumbing is the e4df dependency's CONTRACT — consumed here, not
  re-built, and asserted BEHAVIORALLY at story start by the dependency-gate AC (rather than
  taken on faith): the envelope-bearing `SIGNATURE` event, the reducer passthrough of
  `envelope`/`principal`/bound fields into `attestations[kind]`, and `opcert_from_record`
  read-back. The envelope-bearing record RETAINS the `manifest` list exactly as the
  legacy record carries it (envelope and manifest coexist on the e4df record), so
  `compute_validity`'s manifest-derived checks (`manifest_deps` / `manifest_material` /
  `manifest_regver` / `signing.verified_at_sha_from_manifest`) read it unchanged.
  Validity-on-read lifecycle rules (reopen, code drift,
  material drift) apply to envelope attestations identically. Worked example of the shape:
  `attestations["plan-review"]` holds `{algorithm: "sshsig", envelope: "<encoded DSSE>",
  material_fingerprint: "<hex>", merged_log_commit: "<sha>", principal: "<env_id>"}` (no HMAC
  hex), where `merged_log_commit` is the commit the gate verified against — the manifest's
  `verified-at-sha` line (the seam's bound-commit derivation above; the e4df event already
  carries the field) — and `verify_signature(ticket, kind="plan-review")` returns its existing
  result shape with `certified: true` and the op-cert verdict string (`certified`) — the exact
  field contract is pinned by the existing `verify_signature_result.schema.json`.
- **Downstream `signature`-field consumers are enumerated and updated** (they would otherwise
  break or mis-report on envelope records):
  - `src/rebar/signing.py:648` — the `rebar sign` text-mode report hardcodes
    `record['signature'][:16]`; updated to render the envelope digest + principal for op-cert
    records (no `KeyError`);
  - `src/rebar/_engine_support/validate.py` `signature_findings()` — checks
    `record.get('signature')` and would report envelope-bearing records as `unsigned`; updated
    to route through the same `verify_record` shape dispatch.
- **Output-schema contract** (`docs/output-schemas.md` seam): `sign_result.schema.json` — and
  the `SignResultOut` Pydantic model that the MCP `sign_manifest` tool validates against
  (`src/rebar/_mcp_writes.py:245`) and that `rebar sign --output json` conforms to — currently
  REQUIRES a `signature` (HMAC hex) field; the op-cert record carries `envelope` instead. `verify_signature_result.schema.json`'s CLOSED `verdict` enum (currently `["unsigned",
  "foreign_key", "certified", "mismatch", "key_not_valid_at_era"]`) is widened with EXACTLY the
  four strings the op-cert dispatch path can newly emit — `"invalid"`, `"unavailable"`
  (`SshsigScheme.verify`, sshsig.py:110/125), `"unknown_kind"`, `"unknown_scheme"`
  (`registry.verify`, registry.py:100/107); `certified`/`mismatch`/`key_not_valid_at_era` are
  already present — a backward-compatible enum extension, with the schema-drift test
  regenerated. The sign-result
  shape is updated in ALL THREE of its artifacts together: `sign_result.schema.json`,
  `_mcp_models.py::SignResultOut` (`signature` required → optional alongside `envelope`), and
  the `types.py::SignResult` TypedDict (lines 215-224; `signature: str` becomes
  `NotRequired`). Expand-phase, the schema admits BOTH result shapes (the legacy HMAC shape
  and the envelope shape `{algorithm: "sshsig", envelope, principal, material_fingerprint,
  merged_log_commit, …}` with `signature` no longer required); the contract story (8f1d) later
  tightens it to envelope-only.
- **Degrade, never wedge**: if `ssh-keygen` (OpenSSH ≥ 8.9, `sshsig.ensure_available()`) is
  unavailable or the key is unreadable and cannot be regenerated, signing records the existing
  in-band `{signed: false, error: …}` outcome; no local operation is blocked by signing itself.
  A project whose claim/close gate requires the signature then blocks at that gate with a clear
  install-OpenSSH remediation message.

## Scope
- In: the global `sign_manifest` seam repoint + the five enumerated call sites; key genesis +
  principal resolution; expand-phase read path; the `sign_result.schema.json` / `SignResultOut`
  contract update; tests below.
- Out: HMAC removal (the contract story, 8f1d); the remote service and its client command
  (ee0b); AWS IaC (the deploy story); any change to gate YAMLs or verdict schemas.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Dependency gate (behavioral): on the branch this story builds on (the e4df stack has
      landed), the import check `python -c "from rebar.signing import sign_opcert_manifest; from rebar.attest.opcert import opcert_from_record"`
      succeeds AND a round-trip passes — `sign_opcert_manifest` on a fixture ticket yields a
      reduced state whose `attestations[kind]` carries `envelope`/`principal`/bound fields and
      `opcert_from_record` returns the envelope; recorded as the story's first verification
      step (this asserts e4df's reducer/event contract instead of claiming it).
- [ ] First signing use in a fresh environment generates `<tracker>/.opcert-key`(+`.pub`),
      passphrase-free Ed25519, mode 0600, git-ignored, via the body's exclusive-create mechanism (0700 staging dir, `os.link` of
      the private key as the sole commit point, derivative `.pub` — never `os.replace`); a test
      asserts genesis, permissions, exclusive-create under a concurrent first-sign race (exactly
      one keypair survives; the loser adopts it — no overwrite), and that verify-side resolution
      never creates a key.
- [ ] A passing plan review produces an envelope-bearing `SIGNATURE` event (no HMAC hex) whose
      DSSE subject binds `{ticket_id, material_fingerprint, verified-at sha, kind="plan-review"}`;
      a test asserts `verify_signature(kind="plan-review")` certifies it in the same environment
      and that the never-sign guards (non-PASS / degraded coverage) still refuse to sign.
- [ ] Drift-refresh and resign re-sign as op-certs: tests drive `refresh_attestation` and
      `resign_plan_review` and assert the resulting record still carries a DSSE envelope (a
      refresh never downgrades an op-cert to an HMAC record).
- [ ] The MCP `sign_manifest` tool and the `rebar sign` CLI emit envelope-bearing events via the
      same seam; a test drives each end-to-end and asserts the DSSE envelope is present.
- [ ] A gated close produces an envelope-bearing `SIGNATURE` event (kind `completion-verifier`)
      via the same seam; a test asserts the material-drift recheck still withholds signing on
      drift and `certifiable: false` still closes unsigned.
- [ ] Expand-phase read-both: a test asserts a legacy HMAC attestation written before this change
      still verifies/reads as today, alongside a new envelope attestation on the same ticket
      (kind-keyed coexistence per the additive attestations model).
- [ ] `REBAR_OPCERT_ENV_ID` overrides the DSSE principal; absent, the principal equals the
      environment's `env_id`; a test asserts both.
- [ ] With `ssh-keygen` unavailable (patched `ensure_available`), or the key file unreadable
      AND regeneration impossible (unwritable tracker dir), a
      plan-review PASS records the in-band `{signed: false, …}` outcome and the operation exits
      successfully; a test asserts the claim gate then reports the missing-attestation block
      with a remediation that names OpenSSH ≥ 8.9.
- [ ] `sign_result.schema.json` and `SignResultOut` admit the envelope-bearing result shape
      (and, expand-phase, still admit the legacy HMAC shape); a test validates a real op-cert
      sign result against the schema, and the MCP `sign_manifest` tool returns it without
      validation error.
- [ ] Read-path dispatch: a test asserts the shape-aware wrapper (`verify_attestation_record`)
      routes an envelope-bearing record to the op-cert verifier and a `signature`-bearing record
      to the unchanged `verify_record` HMAC path, on the same store.
- [ ] Consumer regressions: tests assert `rebar sign` text-mode output renders an op-cert
      record without error, and `_engine_support/validate.py` `signature_findings()` does not
      report a certified envelope-bearing record as unsigned.
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` green.
compiled_state.title: op-cert: producer signing seam — gates mint Ed25519 op-certs with the environment key (expand phase)
